tournament.stage_now: As of July 13, 2026, the tournament is in the break between the quarterfinals and semifinals. The quarterfinals concluded July 11 and all four semifinalists are set: France, Spain, England, and Argentina. Semifinal 1 (France vs Spain) is tomorrow, July 14 in Dallas; Semifinal 2 (England vs Argentina) is July 15 in Atlanta. The semifinals have NOT been played yet. Third-place match is July 18 (Miami) and the final is July 19 (MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, NJ).
tournament.completed_summary:
GROUP STAGE (June 11-27, 12 groups of 4; top two per group plus 8 best third-place teams advanced to a new Round of 32): The 32 qualifiers were Argentina, Brazil, France, Spain, England, Portugal, Germany, Netherlands, Belgium, Croatia, Switzerland, Austria, Norway, Sweden, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Mexico, USA, Canada, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, Morocco, Egypt, Algeria, Senegal, Ghana, Ivory Coast, DR Congo, Cape Verde, South Africa, Japan, Australia. The 16 eliminated: South Korea and Czechia (Group A), Qatar (B), Scotland and Haiti (C), Turkiye (D), Curacao (E), Tunisia (F), Iran and New Zealand (G), Uruguay and Saudi Arabia (H), Iraq (I), Jordan (J), Uzbekistan (K), Panama (L). Biggest group-stage surprises: Uruguay (highest-ranked team eliminated, out on 2 points after a Fernando Muslera error gifted Spain a 1-0 win), South Korea's and Turkiye's exits, Cape Verde (smallest nation ever at a World Cup) drawing with Spain, and Curacao taking a point off Ecuador. Paraguay lost its opener 4-1 to the USA but recovered to advance as a third-place team.

ROUND OF 32 (June 28-July 3): Canada 1-0 South Africa; Brazil 2-1 Japan; Paraguay 1-1 Germany (4-3 pens) - Germany, out on penalties, one of the biggest upsets in World Cup knockout history; Morocco 1-1 Netherlands (3-2 pens, after an added-time Morocco equalizer); Norway 2-1 Ivory Coast; France 3-0 Sweden; Mexico 2-0 Ecuador; England 2-1 DR Congo; Belgium 3-2 Senegal (AET); USA 2-0 Bosnia and Herzegovina; Spain 3-0 Austria; Portugal 2-1 Croatia; Switzerland 2-0 Algeria; Egypt 1-1 Australia (4-2 pens); Argentina 3-2 Cape Verde (AET - the debutants leveled twice and were only beaten by Cristian Romero's deflected header from a Messi corner in the 111th minute); Colombia 1-0 Ghana.

ROUND OF 16 (July 4-7): Morocco 3-0 Canada; France 1-0 Paraguay; Norway 2-1 Brazil (Erling Haaland brace in the 79th and ~84th minutes; Bruno Guimaraes had an early penalty saved and Neymar's stoppage-time penalty was consolation - five-time champions out); England 3-2 Mexico; Spain 1-0 Portugal; Belgium 4-1 USA (De Ketelaere x2, Vanaken, Lukaku; Tillman briefly equalized - all three co-hosts eliminated in this round); Argentina 3-2 Egypt; Switzerland 0-0 Colombia (4-3 pens).

QUARTERFINALS (July 9-11): France 2-0 Morocco (Mbappe and Dembele scored, Mbappe also had a penalty saved; rematch of the 2022 semifinal); Spain 2-1 Belgium (teenager Pau Cubarsi's ~30-meter strike past an injured Thibaut Courtois decided it); England 2-1 Norway AET (Andreas Schjelderup gave Norway an early lead; Jude Bellingham equalized before halftime and scored the extra-time winner); Argentina 3-1 Switzerland AET (Mac Allister 10', Ndoye equalized 67', Julian Alvarez won it with a 112th-minute golazo from outside the box).

SEMIFINALS: Not yet played as of July 13.
tournament.remaining_schedule: Semifinal 1: France vs Spain - Tuesday, July 14, 2026, 3:00 PM ET (2:00 PM local / 19:00 GMT), Dallas Stadium (AT&T Stadium, Arlington, TX). Semifinal 2: England vs Argentina - Wednesday, July 15, 2026, 3:00 PM ET (19:00 GMT), Atlanta Stadium (Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta, GA). Third-place match: Saturday, July 18, 2026, 5:00 PM ET, Hard Rock Stadium, Miami, FL. Final: Sunday, July 19, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, MetLife Stadium (New York New Jersey Stadium), East Rutherford, NJ - featuring the first-ever World Cup final halftime show (Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, Burna Boy, BTS).
storylines: Chalk at the top, chaos underneath: all four semifinalists (France, Spain, England, Argentina) were the top pre-tournament favorites. Spain and France opened as +450 co-favorites; France is now the solo favorite at +140 (England +310, Spain +320 per FanDuel as of July 13), having outscored opponents 16-2 across six games with Mbappe, Dembele, and Olise all producing.

Germany's historic humiliation: knocked out by Paraguay on penalties (1-1, 4-3) in the Round of 32 - ranked among the biggest knockout upsets in World Cup history, extending Germany's misery after group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022. Paraguay had lost its opener 4-1 to the USA before rallying to advance as a third-place qualifier, then took France to a 1-0 game in the R16.

Brazil out in the Round of 16 to Norway 2-1: Haaland's late brace (his 6th and 7th goals of the tournament) at MetLife Stadium sank the five-time champions; Bruno Guimaraes missed an early penalty and Neymar's stoppage-time penalty came too late.

Norway's Cinderella run: at their first World Cup since 1998, they beat Ivory Coast, stunned Brazil, and led England in the quarterfinal through Schjelderup before falling 2-1 in extra time to Jude Bellingham's double. Haaland leaves with 7 goals and a live Golden Boot claim.

Messi's last dance and the all-time record: at 39, Messi has 8 goals, became the World Cup's all-time top scorer (passing Klose's 16 with 21 career WC goals), and set a record by scoring in seven consecutive World Cup matches. Argentina's title defense has been pure drama: extra time vs Cape Verde (Romero 111' winner off a Messi corner), 3-2 over Egypt, and Julian Alvarez's 112th-minute golazo vs Switzerland.
Golden Boot photo finish: Mbappe 8 goals (leads Messi's 8 on assists), Haaland 7 (eliminated), Kane 6 and still alive - potentially decided in the semifinals/final between three all-time greats.

Cape Verde, the people's champion: the smallest nation ever to play at a World Cup drew with Spain in the group stage, then twice leveled against defending champion Argentina in the Round of 32 before losing 3-2 in extra time - nearly the biggest upset in tournament history.

Host-nation heartbreak in triplicate: all three co-hosts were eliminated in the Round of 16 within 24 hours - Canada 0-3 Morocco, Mexico 2-3 England, and Pochettino's USA humbled 1-4 by Belgium (De Ketelaere x2, Vanaken, Lukaku) after the USMNT's promising group stage that included the 4-1 win over Paraguay.

Penalty-shootout carnage: four shootouts in the knockout rounds - Paraguay 4-3 Germany (R32), Morocco 3-2 Netherlands (R32, after a Morocco added-time equalizer), Egypt 4-2 Australia (R32), and Switzerland 4-3 Colombia (R16, 0-0) - with Germany and the Netherlands as the marquee victims.

Morocco and Belgium's contrasting deep runs: Morocco again reached the last eight (beating the Netherlands on penalties and thrashing Canada 3-0) before losing the 2022-semifinal rematch to France 2-0; Belgium's revival run (Senegal AET, 4-1 over the USA) ended when 19-year-old Pau Cubarsi beat an injured Courtois from ~30 meters in Spain's 2-1 quarterfinal win. Also notable: Uruguay's group-stage exit on a Muslera error was the single biggest group-stage shock, and Ronaldo's Portugal and Modric's Croatia bowed out in the R16 (0-1 Spain) and R32 (1-2 Portugal) respectively.

contract_families.example_tickers: Series KXMENWORLDCUP ('Men's World Cup winner'); event KXMENWORLDCUP-26 ('2026 FIFA World Cup Winner') with 48 per-country binary markets, e.g. KXMENWORLDCUP-26-FR (France, last 39.6c, volume_fp 63.5M contracts, OI 39.3M), KXMENWORLDCUP-26-AR, KXMENWORLDCUP-26-US. Related futures: KXWCFINALMATCHUP (final matchup), KXWC3RDPLACE, KXWCCONTINENT (winning continent), KXWC1STTIMEWIN (first-time winner), KXWCNOEURSA (non-Europe/South-America winner), KXWCFINISHINGORDER.
contract_families.notes: Listed May 2025 (open_time 2025-05-15, i.e. >1 year before the tournament). Eliminated countries settle No early and finalize (e.g. KXMENWORLDCUP-26-BR settled 'no' 2026-07-05); winner leg resolves when FIFA crowns champion (expected expiry 2026-07-19). Summed event volume via API: ~1.17 billion contracts traded, ~843M open interest as of 2026-07-13. Press: winner market passed $1B traded by July 8 2026 (peaking >$2M/min), second-largest sports market ever on Kalshi behind Super Bowl LX ($2.8B/week). Settlement per FIFA official result; source agencies ESPN/Fox Sports for match-level series.

contract_families.example_tickers: KXWCGAME = 3-way regulation-time moneyline per fixture, e.g. event KXWCGAME-26JUL14FRAESP ('France vs Spain: Regulation Time Moneyline') with legs -FRA (42c), -ESP (30c), -TIE (30c); KXWCGAME-26JUL15ENGARG (semifinal). Companion per-match series: KXWCADVANCE (to advance in KO round), KXWCSPREAD (handicap), KXWCTOTAL (match total goals), KXWCSCORE (correct score), KXWCBTTS (both teams to score), KXWC1H/KXWC2H (half winners), KXWC1HTOTAL/KXWC2HTOTAL, KXWC1HSPREAD/KXWC2HSPREAD, KXWC1HSCORE, KXWCMOV (method of victory), KXWCWINMARGIN, KXWCTTSF/KXWCFTTS (team to score first), KXWCCONCEDE1ST, KXWCMOF (method of finish).
contract_families.notes: Every fixture gets a 3-way (win/draw/loss) contract set — event ticker pattern KXWCGAME-26<MONDD><TEAM1><TEAM2>. Heavily traded: the Argentina–Switzerland R16 game (KXWCGAME-26JUL11ARGSUI) did ~31.3M contracts across its 3 legs. Settled 'Regulation Time' (90 min); KXWCADVANCE covers extra-time/penalty outcomes. In-play props per match/team/player: KXWCCORNERS/KXWCTCORNERS (corners), KXWCSHOT/KXWCSOG/KXWCTEAMSHOT/KXWCTEAMSOG (shots / shots on goal), KXWCSAVE (saves), KXWCGOALCOUNT, KXWCSTART (starters).

contract_families.example_tickers: KXWCGROUPWIN ('World Cup Group Winner') — events KXWCGROUPWIN-26A … KXWCGROUPWIN-26L (one per group, e.g. 'Group L Winner'); KXWCWINGROUP = themed group props (KXWCWINGROUP-26HOST 'Any Host Nation to Win their Group', -26SA, -26ISLAND, -26ASOC); KXWCGROUPQUAL (draw/group assignment, e.g. KXWCGROUPQUAL-26L-ENG settled yes); KXWCGROUPORDER (exact group order), KXWCGROUPBOTTOM (bottom finisher), KXWCGROUPPTS (group points), KXWCGROUPGOALS/KXWCGSGOALS (group goals), KXWC3RDPLACEQUAL (3rd-place qualification), KXWCGROUPWINELIM (group winners eliminated in R32), KXWCGSUNDEFEATED.
contract_families.notes: 48-team/12-group format gives 12 group-winner events plus advancement markets (KXWCADVANCE also covers advancing from group). KXWCGROUPQUAL markets settled at the Dec 2025 draw and remain queryable via the API. Group markets are modest volume relative to winner/match markets (e.g. KXWCGROUPQUAL-26L-ENG ~75K contracts).

contract_families.family: Team-performance props (stage of elimination, host-nation performance)
contract_families.example_tickers: KXWCSTAGEOFELIM per team, e.g. KXWCSTAGEOFELIM-26USA ('USA: Stage of Elimination'), -26URU, -26UZB; KXWCROUND ('Reach Round'), KXWCSTAGE ('Furthest Stage Advanced'); host-nation props: KXWCHOSTWIN-26 ('Any Host Nation to Win the World Cup'), KXWCBESTHOST ('Best Performing Host Nation'), KXWCHOSTSTAGE ('Furthest Stage Advanced to by a Host Nation'), KXWCHOSTKO ('All Host Nations to Reach Knockout Round'); regional: KXWCREGIONKO, KXWCFURTHESTADVANCING; also KXWCUSAOPPONENT ('World Cup USA Opponent'), KXWCTEAMTOTALGOALS ('Tournament Goals' per team), KXWCGOALSALLOWED, KXWCTEAMH2H.
contract_families.notes: CBS Sports confirms 'stage of elimination' as a flagship family. Host-nation (USA/Mexico/Canada) performance is its own cluster of series. Per-team markets settle progressively as teams are eliminated.

contract_families.family: Player props (Golden Boot / top scorer, awards, player goals)
contract_families.example_tickers: KXWCGOALLEADER-26 ('Golden Boot Winner') — 54 player markets, e.g. KXWCGOALLEADER-26-KMBA (Mbappe, 61c, 2.6M contracts), -LMES (Messi, 32c), -EHAA (Haaland), -CRON (Ronaldo); KXWCTEAMLEADGOAL ('World Cup Team Top Scorer', per-country goal leader); KXWCPLAYERGOALS (player tournament goals), KXWCAST (assists), KXWCSOA (score or assist), KXWCHATTRICK, KXWCAWARD/KXWCAWARDCOMBO (tournament awards), KXWCGBOOTGOALS (Golden Boot winner's total), KXWCMBAPPEGOALLEADER (all-time record chase), KXWCMESSIMBAPPE / KXWCMESSIRONALDO (combined-star props), KXWCSQUAD, KXWCGOLDENBOOTCLEAT.
contract_families.notes: Press coverage cites 32 Golden Boot player contracts at launch; the live event now carries 54 player legs (~17M contracts total event volume as of 2026-07-13), plus 32 country goal-leader markets. Settlement on official FIFA award/stat results.

contract_families.example_tickers: KXWCTOTALGOAL — events KXWCTOTALGOAL-26FT ('FIFA World Cup: Total Goals', ladder markets e.g. -26FT-290 '290+ goals', ~328K contracts), -26GS (group-stage total), per-group totals (-26GROUPK); KXWCFASTESTGOAL, KXWCGAMEGOALS (highest-scoring game), KXWCGOALEVERYGAME, KXWCEVERYTEAMGOAL, KXWCKOPENALTIES (matches going to penalties), KXWCLONGESTPEN (longest shootout), KXWCGOALIEGOAL/KXWCGOALIEPEN (goalkeeper goals), KXWCOLIMPICO (olimpico goal), KXWCFREEKICKGOAL, KXWCRECORD/KXWCGOALRECORD (record-breaking), KXWCWINNERTRAIL, KXWCDELAY (match delays), KXWCLOCATION, KXWCFIRSTSONG, KXWCPREPACK (prepackaged parlays-style bundles).
contract_families.notes: About 80 distinct KXWC* series exist in the Sports category (out of 2,366 sports series total on the exchange). All carry Sports category, quadratic fee type, ESPN/Fox Sports settlement sources. Press: >$7.4B traded across all World Cup markets by July 8, 2026, with 'hundreds' of contracts spanning winner, match outcomes, stage of elimination, Golden Boot, and country goal-leader markets.

api_details.auth: Public market data requires NO API key. Verified empirically on 2026-07-13 with unauthenticated curl against https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2/ — /series, /events, /markets, /markets/{ticker}, /markets/trades, /series/{series}/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks, /historical/cutoff and /historical/trades all returned full data with no credentials. Docs show empty security array on GetMarketCandlesticks. API-key auth (RSA-signed headers) is only needed for portfolio/order endpoints. Docs list base URL https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 (demo: external-api.demo.kalshi.co); the api.elections.kalshi.com host serves the same v2 API.
api_details.granularity: Candlesticks: period_interval of 1, 60, or 1440 minutes (1-minute is finest). Max 5,000 candlesticks per request (verified via API error: 'requested time range with candlesticks: 43200, max candlesticks: 5000' — so ~3.47 days of 1-min data per call; paginate with start_ts/end_ts). History depth = full market lifetime: verified 1-min candles retrievable from June 2025 (13 months back) and daily candles back to the winner market's 2025-05-16 open (424 daily candles). Candles are sparse (only periods with activity/quotes returned). Candle fields: end_period_ts, price OHLC + mean/previous (dollars, deci-cent precision), yes_bid/yes_ask OHLC, volume_fp, open_interest_fp. Note: prices/volumes now come as fixed-point dollar strings (*_dollars, *_fp fields); legacy integer-cent fields return null. Trades: tick-by-tick via GetTrades, limit max 1000/request (1001 rejected), cursor pagination, min_ts/max_ts filters, full trade history from market inception.
api_details.historical_endpoints: Live endpoints: GET /markets/trades (GetTrades, tick-level, public); GET /series/{series_ticker}/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks (GetMarketCandlesticks); batch variant BatchGetMarketCandlesticks; GET /events/{ticker}/candlesticks (event-level). Kalshi partitions data into live (~3-month target window) and historical tiers for markets, candlesticks, trades, orders (not events/series). Historical tier (same base URL, also public): GET /historical/cutoff (returns market_settled_ts / trades_created_ts / orders_updated_ts — all 2026-05-14T00:00:00Z as of 2026-07-13), GET /historical/markets, GET /historical/markets/{ticker}, GET /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks, GET /historical/trades, /historical/fills, /historical/orders. Anything older than the cutoff must be fetched from /historical/*; /historical/trades verified working unauthenticated.
api_details.rate_limits: Token-bucket system with separate read/write budgets; most requests cost 10 tokens. Tiers (read/write tokens per second): Basic 200/100 (i.e. ~20 GET requests/sec, automatic on signup), Advanced 300/300 (free upgrade via API call), Expert 600/600, Premier 1000/1000, Paragon 2000/2000, Prime 4000/4000, Prestige 6000/8000 (higher tiers earned via 30-day trading volume or assigned by Kalshi). Burst up to 2x per-second budget after 2 idle seconds. Docs do not state a separate limit for unauthenticated requests; unauthenticated pulls worked at normal speeds in testing.
api_details.settled_market_access: Yes — full history of settled/closed markets remains pullable. Verified: KXMENWORLDCUP-26-BR (settled 'no' 2026-07-05) still returns market data and complete tick trades from the live endpoints; finalized match markets (e.g. KXWCGAME-26JUL11ARGSUI legs) return volume and are candlestick-queryable; Dec-2025-settled qualifier markets (KXWCGROUPQUAL) still enumerate via /markets?status=settled. Markets settled BEFORE the rolling cutoff (currently 2026-05-14, i.e. ~2 months trailing) migrate to /historical/markets and /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks — data is retained, just served from the historical tier. Practical implication for World Cup research: pull candlesticks/trades within ~2-3 months of settlement via live endpoints, after that via /historical/*; nothing observed to be deleted.

sources: https://deadspin.com/prediction-markets/trending/kalshi-world-cup-winner-forecasts-top-1b-in-trading-volume/ (winner market >$1B by 2026-07-08; $7.4B all WC markets; 2nd-largest Kalshi sports market after Super Bowl LX's $2.8B)
https://www.cbssports.com/prediction/news/kalshi-world-cup-2026/ (contract families: winner, 3-way match outcomes, stage of elimination, 32 Golden Boot + 32 country goal-leader markets)

odds_sources.name: The Odds API (the-odds-api.com)
odds_sources.access: REST API. Historical endpoints: /v4/historical/sports/{sport}/odds (snapshots) and /historical/events/{id}/odds. Sport keys include soccer_fifa_world_cup (match odds) and a World Cup winner outrights key. Historical endpoints are paid-plan only.
odds_sources.cost: Paid plans start around $30/mo; historical requests cost 10 credits x markets x regions (vs 1 for live), so a full tournament time-series pull needs a mid/high tier. See the-odds-api.com/historical-odds-data/ for plan details.
odds_sources.historical_depth: TRUE TIME SERIES, retrievable today. Featured markets (h2h/1X2, spreads, totals) archived since June 6, 2020 at 10-min snapshots; 5-min snapshots since Sept 2022; additional markets since May 3, 2023. Their FIFA World Cup page confirms historical 1X2 available from April 2022, covering all WC26 matches played so far. Caveat: outright-winner futures only appear in the archive from the date the outright sport key was added to the live API - verify coverage window before relying on it for the full qualifying period.
odds_sources.notes: Best single programmatic source: includes Pinnacle and Betfair Exchange prices alongside DraftKings/FanDuel/bet365 etc., so one API yields bookmaker AND exchange time series to line up against Kalshi. Docs: the-odds-api.com/liveapi/guides/v4/ (note: pre-Sept-2022 American odds are converted from decimal with small rounding errors).

odds_sources.name: Betfair Exchange Historical Data (historicdata.betfair.com)
odds_sources.access: Purchase-and-download portal plus a bulk-download Historical Data API; requires a registered Betfair account (Betfair is not licensed in the US, so account creation needs a supported jurisdiction). Files are per-market JSON streams; Betfair provides a JSON-to-CSV converter.
odds_sources.cost: Basic tier is FREE (1-minute intervals, last-traded price only, no volume). Advanced (1-second, top-3 price ladder + volume) and Pro (50ms full ladder + volume) are paid; prices are quoted per sport/month inside the site after login (not published publicly).
odds_sources.historical_depth: TRUE traded-price time series for nearly all Exchange markets since 2016, including WC26 match odds and the outright winner market. Data is released on a monthly cycle after each month closes: June 2026 (group stage) should be purchasable now; the July knockout rounds and final land after July ends - a real timing constraint if the piece publishes right after the July 19 final.
odds_sources.notes: Gold standard for genuine market-clearing prices (traded volume, BSP, weighted averages) - the fairest apples-to-apples comparison to Kalshi contract prices. Docs/FAQ: developer.betfair.com/historical-data-services-api/ and betfair-datascientists.github.io/data/usingHistoricDataSite/. Spec PDF: historicdata.betfair.com/Betfair-Historical-Data-Feed-Specification.pdf.

odds_sources.name: OddsPortal (oddsportal.com)
odds_sources.access: Free website only - no official API. WC26 results/archive pages exist now (oddsportal.com/football/world/world-cup-2026/results/). Retrieval means scraping (WebHarvy and similar tools are commonly used); scraping is against their ToS - a consideration for a journalism piece.
odds_sources.historical_depth: SNAPSHOTS, not a time series: opening and closing odds per match per bookmaker (1X2, O/U, AH), back to 1998 World Cups. Hover popups show limited intermediate movement but it is not bulk-retrievable. Fine for open-vs-close analysis, not for tracking intraday moves against Kalshi.
odds_sources.notes: Useful free cross-check on closing lines across dozens of books; cite carefully given scraping ToS.

odds_sources.name: Oddschecker (oddschecker.com)
odds_sources.access: Live odds-comparison site; no public historical archive and no public API. Historical WC26 odds are NOT retrievable retrospectively - you could only have captured them while live.
odds_sources.cost: Free to view current odds; commercial B2B data feeds exist by negotiation.
odds_sources.notes: Effectively a dead end for retrospective time series; mention only as a live comparison surface (oddschecker.com/football/world-cup).

odds_sources.name: Pinnacle (direct + third-party archives)
odds_sources.access: Pinnacle's own API requires a funded account and B2B approval, and is not available to US residents; it serves live odds, not a deep public archive. Practical routes to historical Pinnacle lines: (a) The Odds API archive (Pinnacle is in its EU-region books), (b) api.bettingiscool.com - a dedicated Pinnacle historical-odds archive with years of sharp closing lines (~2.7B odds records back to 2021 per aggregator marketing), (c) OddsPapi (oddspapi.io) - free-tier JSON API with full price history per fixture since January 2026, Pinnacle included (max 3 bookmakers per request, 1 request/5s cooldown), (d) sportsapis.dev historical closing-line products.
odds_sources.cost: Direct API: free with funded account (where legal). bettingiscool: paid subscription. OddsPapi: free tier covers historical since Jan 2026. The Odds API: as above.
odds_sources.historical_depth: Via third parties: genuine time series (every price change with timestamps on OddsPapi; 5-min snapshots on The Odds API) covering the whole WC26 tournament. Pinnacle closing lines are the standard 'efficient market' benchmark for judging whether Kalshi prices were sharp.
odds_sources.notes: For the piece, Pinnacle de-vigged closing probabilities vs Kalshi contract prices is the most defensible bookmaker comparison (1-3% overround). Also free: Covers' Sports Odds History (covers.com/sportsoddshistory/soccer-odds/) archives soccer futures odds including a WC26 page - free with attribution, though snapshot frequency is unspecified.

prediction_markets.name: Polymarket
prediction_markets.access: Two free, no-auth public APIs: Gamma API (gamma-api.polymarket.com) for market metadata, volume and liquidity; CLOB API prices-history endpoint (clob.polymarket.com/prices-history, docs: docs.polymarket.com/developers/CLOB/timeseries) for price time series per outcome token (params: market=token_id, startTs/endTs or interval up to 'max', fidelity in minutes, default 1-min).
prediction_markets.wc26_coverage: Massive: ~203 active FIFA World Cup markets as of July 9, 2026; the World Cup Winner market alone has ~$4.1B lifetime volume (reported by Deadspin). Match markets, advancement, Golden Boot, plus the flagship outright. polymarket.com/fifa-world-cup is the hub.
prediction_markets.notes: Historical time series IS retrievable today for any token, free. Practical caveats: long lookbacks are served at coarser fidelity, so pull minute-level data in chunked startTs/endTs windows; a known GitHub issue (Polymarket/py-clob-client #189) documents occasional blank responses on historical queries. Alternates: polymarketanalytics.com (hosted historical charts/analytics), Apify Polymarket WC26 scraper (paid), and Neil Paine's free WC26 odds tracker built on Polymarket data (neilpaine.substack.com/p/2026-world-cup-odds-tracker). Oddpool (oddpool.com/explore/world-cup) already juxtaposes Kalshi and Polymarket WC26 prices - useful for spot-checking your own joins.

prediction_markets.name: Kalshi (the subject - its own history is retrievable)
prediction_markets.access: Trade API v2, no auth needed for market data: GetMarketCandlesticks (1-min / 1-hour / 1-day OHLC of yes bid/ask plus volume and open interest; docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market-candlesticks); GET /historical/trades for tick-level trade history (cursor pagination, 100 rows/page - full history requires thousands of requests); markets settled before a cutoff live under /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks. kalshi.com/market-data is the official data hub.
prediction_markets.wc26_coverage: Kalshi is the Official Prediction Market Partner of FIFA World Cup 26. Winner series ticker kxmenworldcup (kalshi.com/markets/kxmenworldcup/mens-world-cup-winner/kxmenworldcup-26), 3-way match markets for every fixture, 32 Golden Boot and team-goal-leader series; reported daily volume above $1B since June 11.
prediction_markets.notes: Full minute-level candlestick history for every WC26 contract is retrievable today, free. If bulk convenience is worth paying for, Lychee Data sells prepackaged Kalshi trade/orderbook history (36GB+, CSV/JSON; lycheedata.com/kalshi-historical-data).

prediction_markets.name: Manifold Markets (play-money)
prediction_markets.access: Free, open API with full bet and probability history per market; official @ManifoldSports account runs auto-created, auto-resolving WC26 match markets plus a tournament dashboard (news.manifold.markets/p/changelog-69-official-world-cup-markets).
prediction_markets.wc26_coverage: Every WC26 match plus community prop markets; play-money (mana), so volumes are not dollar-comparable, but it is a clean 'crowd forecast' comparator.
prediction_markets.notes: Historical probability time series fully retrievable today via API at no cost. Frame it as a forecasting crowd, not a real-money market, in the piece.

prediction_markets.name: Metaculus (forecasting community, not a market)
prediction_markets.access: Free API with full community-prediction time series when questions exist.
prediction_markets.wc26_coverage: UNCONFIRMED - searches did not surface dedicated WC26 questions; check metaculus.com directly before counting on it.
prediction_markets.notes: Include only if WC26 questions exist; otherwise drop. PredictIt has no sports markets; Smarkets exchange data was not verified in this pass.

public_opinion.name: YouGov (US)
public_opinion.access: Free articles with toplines and downloadable crosstabs at yougov.com; deeper microdata requires a YouGov relationship. Key WC26 items: 'What Americans think about the 2026 World Cup' (fielded May 21-24, 2026, n=1,096), 'FIFA World Cup 2026 is already outscoring the 2022 edition in the U.S.', a June 15, 2026 daily question on who Americans are rooting for, and a running FIFA World Cup popularity/fame tracker page.
public_opinion.what_it_measures: US interest levels (13% very interested), rooting preferences (65% of interested Americans happy to see US win; Brazil 22%, England 20%, Argentina 18%, Mexico 16%), and expectations - directly comparable to Kalshi's US-heavy user base.
public_opinion.notes: These are discrete survey waves (2-4 snapshots across the tournament), not a continuous series - you can show level differences vs Kalshi prices but not co-movement. All retrievable today.

public_opinion.name: Pew Research Center
public_opinion.access: Free: 'Who do Americans think is going to win the World Cup?' short read published June 2, 2026 (pewresearch.org), with topline and methodology downloadable.
public_opinion.what_it_measures: A nationally representative 'who will win' expectation question - the closest thing to a direct public-opinion analogue of an outright-winner market.
public_opinion.notes: Single pre/early-tournament snapshot; retrievable today. Perfect for a 'crowd vs market' divergence chart at one point in time.

public_opinion.name: Ipsos (global)
public_opinion.access: Free PDFs: Global Advisor 'Predictions 2026' 30-country survey (fielded Oct 24 - Nov 7, 2025, n=23,642) with World Cup winner predictions by country (e.g., 87% of Argentinians pick Argentina vs 25% of Brazilians); country-specific June 2026 World Cup surveys (Jordan, UAE editions); US survey on the WC boosting soccer interest.
public_opinion.what_it_measures: Cross-national home-team bias in winner expectations - a striking contrast against market prices for a data-journalism angle.
public_opinion.notes: Snapshots only (one pre-tournament global wave plus scattered June 2026 country waves); all PDFs retrievable today at ipsos.com.

public_opinion.name: Google Trends
public_opinion.access: Three routes: (1) trends.google.com UI - free, historical search-interest time series (5+ years back, daily/weekly granularity depending on window) downloadable as CSV today, fully retrospective; (2) official Google Trends API - still ALPHA as of mid-2026 (announced July 2025), invite-only with a waitlist application, consistent scaling and 1,800 days of history - do not plan on getting access in time; (3) third-party scrapers (SerpApi, ScrapingBee, Apify) around $50-100/mo for programmatic pulls.
public_opinion.what_it_measures: Public attention per team/player over time - a continuous 'public interest' series you can actually correlate with Kalshi price moves, unlike poll waves.
public_opinion.notes: This is the only public-opinion signal with true retrospective time-series retrievability at match-day granularity. Announcement: developers.google.com/search/blog/2025/07/trends-api.

public_opinion.name: Gallup
public_opinion.access: No WC26 'who will win' polling found as of 2026-07-13.
public_opinion.notes: Searches surfaced nothing WC26-specific from Gallup; treat as unavailable and lean on YouGov/Pew/Ipsos instead.

model_forecasts.name: Opta 'supercomputer' (The Analyst / Stats Perform)
model_forecasts.access: Free web articles only - no public API. Stage-by-stage WC26 articles are all still online: pre-tournament (Spain 16.1% from 25,000 sims), post-group-stage, post-round-of-32 (Spain 21.3%, Argentina 17.38%), pre-quarter-final (France 27.3% to win, 44.3% to reach final). theanalyst.com/articles/who-will-win-2026-fifa-world-cup-predictions-opta-supercomputer and sibling articles.
model_forecasts.notes: Retrievable today as a coarse time series (one probability vector per tournament stage) by harvesting the article set - enough for a 'model vs market' overlay at 5-6 points. SI.com re-reports each update if an article goes missing.

model_forecasts.name: Nate Silver / Silver Bulletin (FiveThirtyEight successor)
model_forecasts.access: natesilver.net (Substack) - 'World Cup 2026: knockout stage predictions' uses the old FiveThirtyEight-style model and heatmap format (Silver retained ownership of the models). Some posts free, full access ~$8-15/mo subscription.
model_forecasts.notes: Snapshots per post, not an API. FiveThirtyEight itself is dead for sports; context piece: fromthebyline.substack.com 'FiveThirtyEight is Dead; Long Live Public Soccer Projection Models'.

model_forecasts.name: World Football Elo Ratings (eloratings.net) + ClubElo
model_forecasts.access: eloratings.net is the national-team Elo source with a dedicated 2026_World_Cup page and per-team rating histories - free but no official API (JS-rendered site; scraping required, e.g., the approach in eddwebster/football_analytics). ClubElo has a clean free CSV API (api.clubelo.com/YYYY-MM-DD, wrapped by the Python 'soccerdata' package) but covers CLUBS only - not usable for national teams, only as a methods reference.
model_forecasts.notes: Elo histories are fully retrievable today, so you can compute your own model-implied win probabilities for any date and compare drift vs Kalshi. Open-source alternative: Hicruben/world-cup-2026-prediction-model on GitHub (Elo + Dixon-Coles + Monte Carlo, live at cup26matches.com) - reproducible probabilities for any tournament date.

model_forecasts.name: Other public WC26 simulators
model_forecasts.access: Football Meets Data (football-md.com/world-cup-simulator, 10k+ Elo-based Monte Carlo sims, free web), PlayerElo squad-Elo predictions (playerelo.football/world-cup-2026), SixOneFive Soccer forecaster (5,000 sims on FIFA/Elo ratings), plus an arXiv preprint (arxiv.org/pdf/2606.24171) with WC26 predictions.
model_forecasts.notes: Mostly current-snapshot websites; historical model output is generally NOT archived except where articles/Wayback captures exist. Use Opta + your own Elo-derived series for time-series comparisons; use these for breadth at a single date.
